Notice And Motion To Intervene; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Anna J. Monteleone
Off Calendar Jul-11-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

INTERVENOR AC TRANSIT's Motion To Intervene.

Proposed Plaintiff-in-Intervention AC Transit's motion to intervene is denied. As a result of the order sustaining the demurrer without leave to amend as to the entirety of the complaint filed by plaintiff Regina Quarles, no useful purpose is served by allowing AC Transit to intervene.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/HEK)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Golden Gate Bridge Highway and Transportation District's demurrer to the entirety of the complaint filed by plaintiff Regina Quarles is sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed and the moving papers show that plaintiff is unable to state a valid claim.

Order is Signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-07-2025 Continued To Aug-06-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Re-Notice Of Hearing On Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4 (California Tort Claims Act)
Off Calendar Sep-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER REGINA QUARLES' Hearing On Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4 (California Tort Claims Act).

Plaintiff's petition for "an order for relief from the provisions of Government Code 945.4," a claim presentation law, is granted.

Plaintiff is an AC Transit bus driver injured in a collision with a Golden Gate bus on April 5, 2023, giving her sixth months to present a claim to Golden Gate - a deadline she missed. On December 29, 2023, plaintiff submitted a request for presentation of a late claim, which Golden Gate denied on February 29, 2024. This petition was then timely filed. (Govt. Code 946.6(c).)

Plaintiff says her "failure to present the timely claim was due to mistake and excusable neglect," so she should be relieved. (Id.; Pet. 5:10-11.) She says the accident was investigated by law enforcement, AC Transit and Golden Gate. She filled out claim forms and believed worker's compensation was her sole remedy. A lawyer she consulted did not disabuse her of this notion or tell her of the six-month claim deadline that she can recall. She learned she might have a claim against Golden Gate when she consulted a second lawyer in December 2023. This "court has broad discretion in ruling on a petition for relief from the claim-filing requirement" and declines to apply the claim presentation laws as rigidly as Golden Gate advocates. (County of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (2001) 91 Cal.App.4th 1303, 1313.)
Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4
Off Calendar Aug-13-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER REGINA QUARLES's Petition To The Court For An Order For Relief From The Provisions Of Government Code 945.4.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The petition for relief from Government Code 945.4 is ordered off calendar for petitioner's noncompliance with Local Rule 2.7(B) (courtesy copies). The petition may be re-set for a Mon.-Thur. after August 29. Petitioner's papers shall bear the new date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
